growth_records.cite: Stuart et al. Cyanobacterial reuse of extracellular organic carbon in microbial mats. The ISME Journal (2016). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2015.180. [PMC5029224]
growth_records.snippet: Growth rate of the cultured biofilm under these experimental conditions was 0.114 d-1 (a doubling time of ~6 days, Supplementary Figure S2).
growth_records.notes: Growth rate reported directly in text as 0.114 d^-1; converted units only (0.114/24 = 0.00475 h^-1). Doubling time reported as ~6 days, converted to hours (6*24=144 h); cross-check: mu=ln2/144h=0.00481 h^-1, consistent with the reported 0.114 d^-1 (0.114/24=0.00475 h^-1). Cultures grown at 23°C, 20 µmol m-2 s-1 light, 12h:12h light:dark cycle, ASN medium (modified ASN-III per Rippka 1988); 3 mM 13C-bicarbonate was an isotope-labeling supplement used to generate labeled biomass, not part of baseline growth medium composition (noted separately).
